propositions: Release actions in release.yml reference mutable major-version tags instead of full 40-char commit SHAs, so identical rebar SHAs can execute different build/publish code.
Release checkouts do not set persist-credentials: false, leaving the token persisted in local git config by default.

Build tools (build, Hatchling, Twine, validation tools) are installed with unrestricted upgrades rather than from a hash-locked constraints file generated by pip-compile --generate-hashes and installed with --require-hashes.
Hatchling's version must be enforced end-to-end via python -m build --no-isolation against a pre-installed hash-locked env rather than a pyproject.toml [build-system] pin.
The MCP publisher is downloaded from a mutable releases/latest URL and executed without digest/signature verification in a job holding id-token: write.

The fix splits publishing into an unprivileged mcp_verify job (download + sha256 check) and an OIDC-privileged mcp_registry job that consumes only the verified artifact, so OIDC scope is never co-resident with an unverified binary.
